data.title: UK's De La Rue receives takeover offer from British financier, Sky News reports
data.description:
Bank note currency printer De La Rue DLAR received a takeover offer from prominent British financier Edi Truell, Sky News reported on Thursday.

Truell has made a 125 pence per share conditional cash offer for the company, the report added.

data.title: Vestas Has Prospects For Higher U.S. Volumes Despite Trump Uncertainty — Market Talk
data.description: Vestas has prospects for higher volumes in the U.S., despite increased uncertainty from the Donald Trump presidency, Jyske Bank's Janne Vincent Kjaer writes. Vestas was hit hard by challenges in the service business in 2024, which, combined with Trump's election victory, has given rise to concerns about growth prospects. The market seems to be factoring in a severe slowdown in the U.S. with Trump putting an end to the expansion of wind power. "However, we do not expect it to be quite that bad." Vestas has a record-high order book in the U.S. which extends all the way into 2027, Kjaer says. Jyske rates Vestas at buy with a 170 Danish kroner price target. Shares trade 0.2% higher at 96.94 kroner. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

data.title: Europe's Gas Price Retreats Further — Market Talk
data.description: European natural-gas prices fall in midday trade, with the benchmark Dutch TTF contract down 2.7% to 44.20 euros a megawatt-hour from over 50 euros just a week ago. "[The] TTF market is experiencing a phenomenal reversal over the last week, which is illustrating the strength of funds positioning in the price formation," Rabobank's energy strategist Florence Schmit says. "The price retreat of around 10% on the week on an improved weather outlook is signalling that last week's rally might have been overdone." The market is currently adjusting to the loss of some gas volumes from Russia after a transit deal with Ukraine expired on Dec. 31. Meanwhile, EU gas storage is now less than 70% full--a level that is below the five-year average and that could pose challenges for the upcoming summer injection season. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: RBC Trims Price Target on Camden Property Trust to $121 From $127, Keeps Sector Perform Rating
data.description: Camden Property Trust CPT has an average rating of hold and mean price target of $127.13, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Market Chatter: Blackstone Plans to Invest $300 Million in AI Data Firm DDN at $5 Billion Valuation
data.description:
Blackstone BX is investing $300 million in California-based artificial intelligence data firm DDN at a $5 billion company valuation, The Wall Street Journal reported Thursday, citing executives from both companies.

Founded in 1998 by Alex Bouzari and Paul Bloch, DDN initially served government agencies and research organizations, managing data for complex projects like NASA missions and drug development simulations.

DDN's Chief Executive Alex Bouzari reportedly said his company is engaged in the sale of computing equipment and software to manage and glean information from large-scale and complex data used in AI models.

Blackstone did not immediately respond to requests for comment from MT Newswires.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: EU Clears Adnoc Drilling, Alpha Dhabi Holding's Joint Ownership of Deep Well Services
data.description:
The European Commission on Thursday approved the joint ownership of US-based Deep Well Services Holdings by United Arab Emirates-based drilling company Adnoc Drilling (ADX:ADNOCDRILL) and investment firm Alpha Dhabi Holding (ADX:ALPHADHABI).

The commission said the transaction, which concerns petroleum and natural gas extraction activities, is not expected to raise competition concerns due to its minimal impact on the European Economic Area.

data.title: Marks & Spencer Stock Weighed by Weaker Demand Fears — Market Talk
data.description: Marks & Spencer's Christmas update was positive, but fears of possible weaker consumer environment weighed on the stock, AlphaValue says in a note. The company flagged a challenging environment and said it expects higher costs from increased taxes. "U.K. retailers anticipate that employer tax increases could impact prices and employment, potentially leading to a softer consumer environment in the months ahead," AlphaValue says. Furthermore, higher cost inflation, particularly rising wage costs, could add pressure on profitability in the year ahead. However, this could be a double-edged sword, as the increase in the National Living Wage in the U.K. could also boost purchasing power, which might support consumer spending, AlphaValue says. Shares are down 6.4% at 353 pence. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

data.title: Commerzbank on Overnight News
data.description:
Commerzbank in its "European Sunrise" note of Thursday highlighted:

Markets: United States Treasuries stabilize after a solid 30-year auction, Japanese government bonds gain after a strong 30-year auction. Equities are mixed. Sterling stays on the back foot, other major foreign exchange crosses little changed. Brent briefly dips below $76/barrel.

Fed: Many officials underlined the need for a "careful approach over coming quarters," citing higher inflation and resilient economy, "almost all" saw increased upside risks to inflation. SOMA manager flags balance sheet challenges from debt limit, banks shift expectations for the end of quantitative tightening to June (minutes).

Trump: Secretary of State Antony Blinken says the US takeover of Greenland will never occur. President-elect Donald Trump's National Security Adviser Mike Waltz says Greenland is important for US national security.

== EUROPE:

ECB: Governing Council (GC) member Francois Villeroy de Galhau says the European Central Bank should keep cutting rates until reaching neutral 2% by summer. December inflation increase was expected. GC member Fabio Cipollone warns against keeping demand low to safeguard against inflation.

France: Villeroy urges government to be as ambitious as possible to tackle "chronic sickness" of public finances, warns of "dangerously" high OAT spreads.

Germany: CDU issues "agenda 2030" aiming for medium-term gross domestic product growth of at least 2%.

Italy: Tesoro announced three- and seven-year taps for Monday's auctions, cancels longer maturity tap amid Wednesday's syndication.

UK: British Finance Minister Rachel Reeves issues a statement saying fiscal rules are "non-negotiable," government will have an "iron grip on public finances." Reeves urges spending cuts if gilt slump erases fiscal headroom (Bloomberg).

UK: BRC prices were down 1% year over year in December while the consensus was for a 0.4%. CBI's business outlook fell at the fastest pace in two years, REC hiring at the fastest pace in 16 months.

UK: Elon Musk examines how Keir Starmer could be removed as British prime minister before the next election (FT).

==ASIA:

China: The consumer price index fell to 0.1% year over year as expected, the producer price index rose to -2.3% while the consensus was for -2.4%.

Japan: Cash earnings rose 3.0% in November while the consensus was 2.7%, the most in 32 years.

data.title: Tesco Backs Profit Target After Christmas Sales Growth — 2nd Update
data.description:
By Michael Susin

Tesco reiterated its profit target for its fiscal year after reporting sales growth during the Christmas period.

The U.K. supermarket chain on Thursday said it continues to expect retail adjusted operating profit--its preferred metric, which strips out exceptional and other one-off items--in the fiscal year ending in February of around 2.9 billion pounds ($3.59 billion). However, the guidance comes below the market expectations of 3.07 billion pounds, according to a company poll of 13 analysts.

Tesco said like-for-like retail sales rose 3.1% on year in the 19 weeks ended Jan. 4. They rose 2.8% in the third quarter and 3.8% over the Christmas period.

Comparable sales in the U.K., where Tesco is the largest grocer with nearly 30% of the market share according to research firm Kantar, rose 3.9% in the 19-week period and 4.1% during the Christmas period. The increase was driven by volume growth across the period, with a robust contribution from fresh food.

Sales of its premium-range offerings--which included more than 300 festive products--increased 15.5% as British shoppers didn't only buy more but were also willing to pay more than usual to treat themselves better. The latest report from Kantar said nine in 10 households bought at least one premium-range product from a supermarket in December.

During an investors call, Chief Executive Ken Murphy and Chief Financial Imran Nawaz said the sales volume was the key driver of growth and Tesco absorbed part of the inflation costs rather than increasing prices on the counter as part of its efforts to face subdued consumer sentiment. The use of volume scale to boost its market position and reduce costs will be continued, they added.

The executives added that the company will move toward a greater personalization through its loyalty card following a positive response during the festivities with the addition of challenges as part of a gamification strategy. The concept is becoming increasingly present in the sector, with grocers encouraging consumer engagement by offering rewards through elements of game playing such as point scores.

They also said that its retail media--a segment of personalized advertising inside the stores that recently became a new stream of revenue--has been very successful and it continues to be in strong growth. Tesco doesn't disclose the individual figures of this segment, but said it is a contributor to profits and it continues to grow.

Tesco continues to expect retail free cash flow for fiscal 2025 within its medium-term guidance range of 1.4 billion to 1.8 billion pounds. It guides for adjusted operating profit contribution from the retained Tesco Bank business of around 120 million pounds.

The stock fell 1.3% to 36.53 pounds in European morning trading alongside peers such as Sainsbury's and Marks & Spencer.

The fall could relate to a tinge of disappointment that guidance wasn't expanded following the festive success, head of markets at Interactive Investor, Richard Hunter, said in a note.

However, AJ Bell's investment director Russ Mould said the lack of guidance upgrade despite sales growth could reflect Tesco's commitment to keeping prices low and having extra staff during Christmas season in order to increase sales by helping shoppers and build brand loyalty. The grocer could be focusing on a strategy of foregoing a short-term boost to benefit from an improved competitive position in the long term, he said.

In the same day, peer Marks & Spencer reported quarterly sales up 8.7%, mainly driven robust by volume growth in food business. The group launched 500 new lines and sales of new Christmas products grew 14%.

Write to Michael Susin at michael.susin@wsj.com

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at close
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex SENSEX fell 0.68% to 77,620.21, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 index NIFTY dropped 0.69% to 23,526.50, as anxiety over a likely moderation in third-quarter corporate earnings sapped investor confidence.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee USDINR hit a record closing low of 85.8475 per U.S. dollar, as the greenback strengthened on expectations of slower-than-anticipated rate cuts by the Federal Reserve as well as uncertainty about President-elect Donald Trump's policies.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN067934G=CC) was quoted at 100.165 rupees, with the yield little changed at 6.7646%, amid consolidation ahead of debt supply and following declines earlier this week as foreign banks piled up debt purchases.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

Both the one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) and the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) were flat, at 6.505% and 6.1975% respectively.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 6.25% and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 6.75%.

On January 8, 2025, Ziccum AB (the “Company”) disclosed a press release withinformation on the Company's financial situation.

The rules of Nasdaq First North Growth Market state that an issuer can be givenobservation status if there is material adverse uncertainty in respect of theissuer's financial position.

data.title: BOE Faces Pressure to Scale Back QT Programme After Gilts Selloff — Market Talk
data.description: The Bank of England could come under pressure to scale back its quantitative easing programme if U.K. government bond yields continue to rise, MUFG Bank's Lee Hardman says in a note. The BOE plans to reduce its gilt holdings by GBP100 billion in a 12-month period from October 2024, including GBP12 billion through active gilt sales and the rest allowing gilts to mature. The selloff in gilts looks mainly driven by a broader repricing of long-end bonds rather than U.K. specific factors, Hardman says. "Having said that the rising cost of U.K. government borrowing if sustained will put pressure on the government to tighten fiscal policy."(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Currencies drift as dollar, weak output keep up pressure
data.description:
Central Europe's currencies struggled for direction on Thursday, hemmed in by a still strong U.S. dollar and weak local industrial output data.

Most currencies in the region have remained under pressure at the start of 2025, and a Reuters poll on Thursday forecast slight losses for the coming year on lingering economic risks, including potential U.S. tariffs.

Data on Thursday showed industrial output falling more than expected in the Czech Republic and Hungary in November.

The Czech crown EURCZK was little changed at 25.12 to the euro at 1021 GMT while Hungary's forint EURHUF had also barely moved at 413.90 to the euro, hovering near Monday's two-year low of 416.50.

"(The forint) saw some relief yesterday, but we see little chance of this being the start of a broader rally," ING said in a trading note, adding that more closures of sizeable short forint positions could generate positive momentum.

"However, we don't see much reason for the market to move in that direction at the moment given that the consensus seems to be heading for higher EUR/HUF levels later," the bank said.

A Reuters poll forecast the forint would fall to 420 per euro by the end of the year. The currency dropped more than 6% in 2024.

Poland's zloty (EURPLN) eased 0.1% to 4.2785 per euro, after moving beyond 4.25 at the start of the week, its strongest since 2020.

The Polish finance ministry sold a series of bonds on Thursday, as yields remain elevated.

A 10-year benchmark bond (PL10YT=RR) was bid around 6%, around its highest yield since October and compared with around 5% midway through 2024.

Thursday's bond sale was taking place in "rather unfavourable circumstances", Alior Bank said, adding that the zloty was also suffering from increased risk aversion and a stronger dollar.

The stronger dollar has weighed on central Europe's currencies, with market focus on U.S. President-elect Donald Trump's agenda as he steps back into the White House on Jan. 20. Analysts expected his policies to bolster U.S. growth and add to price pressures.

data.title: Parkson Retail Unit Renews Tenancy Contract
data.description:
Parkson Retail Group 3368 unit Liupanshui Parkson Retail renewed an agreement to continue the tenancy of a China property leased from Guizhou Hanhua Real Estate Development for 10 years, a Thursday bourse filing said.

The current lease is expiring Dec. 31, 2026, and the new 10-year contract will take effect from Jan. 1, 2027, according to the store, mall, and supermarket operator.

The property comprises parts of Baolong International Building in Liupanshui City, Guizhou Province, China, and is being used by the company for the operation of department stores since 2012.

data.title: Albertsons Is Maintained at Outperform by Telsey Advisory Group
data.description: (11:29 GMT) Albertsons Price Target Maintained With a $26.00/Share by Telsey Advisory Group

data.title: EU Regulator OKs Smartsheet's Acquisition by Vista Equity Partners, Blackstone
data.description:
The European Commission greenlit the joint acquisition of Frankfurt-listed software company Smartsheet SMAR by US investors Vista Equity Partners Management and Blackstone.

The European Union watchdog said Thursday that the transaction, which relates to the collaborative work management software sector, is not expected to raise competition concerns due to the companies' limited combined market position after the merger.

data.title: Teleflex awarded Short Term Central Venous Catheters contract from Vizient
data.description:
WAYNE, Pa., Jan. 09, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Teleflex Incorporated TFX, a leading global provider of medical technologies, today announced it has been awarded a contract by Vizient, Inc., the largest provider-driven healthcare performance improvement company in the U.S. The agreement, effective January 1, 2025, covers the supply of Teleflex’s Central Venous Access Catheters and Arterial Catheters.

Vizient provides solutions and services that improve the delivery of high-value care by aligning cost, quality, and market performance for more than 65% of the nation’s acute care providers. This includes 97% of the nation’s academic medical centers, and more than 35% of the acute care market. Vizient provides expertise, analytics, and advisory services, as well as a contract portfolio that represents more than $140 billion in annual purchasing volume to improve patient outcomes and lower costs. Through this agreement, Vizient provider-customers will now gain access to increased savings and pre-negotiated terms on Teleflex’s vascular access products.

“As the market leader in both Central Venous Catheters (CVCs)1 and Arterials2, we are pleased to offer Vizient provider-customers one of the broadest portfolios of vascular access products,” said Lisa Kudlacz, President and General Manager, Teleflex Vascular. “Arrow™ Vascular Access Products are designed to equally benefit clinicians and patients, help protect against vascular access related complications like infection, thrombosis, and tip malposition, and help clinicians follow independent third-party vascular access guidelines3-6, 8-13.”

For more than four decades, Teleflex, through its Arrow™ Catheters has been at the forefront of vascular access innovation. The Arrowg+ard Blue Plus™ CVCs feature broad-spectrum antimicrobial protection against fungi, gram-positive, and gram-negative bacteria, reducing Central Line-Associated Bloodstream Infections (CLABSI) by 67-100%3-6.

The Catheter-Related Bloodstream Infection (CRBSI) risk rates for Arterial Catheters and short-term CVCs have been shown to be comparable, indicating that Arterials should receive the same precautions for catheter insertion and care.7 Arrow™ Arterial Catheterization Kits provide the essential tools and safety features in an all-inclusive kit—with a layout that is intuitive and easy to use.

The contract includes access to a range of products, including:

  • Arrowg+ard Blue Plus™ CVCs
  • Arrow™ ErgoPack™ Complete Systems
  • Arrow™ Arterial Catheters and Catheterization Kits

These solutions help healthcare systems:

  • Comply with practice recommendations including: CDC Recommendations, SHEA Guidelines, INS Standards of Practice and OSHA Bloodborne Pathogens Standard8-11
  • Standardize vascular access products across a healthcare system
  • Maintain a high standard of patient care
  • Control costs and risks from infections3-6, 12

To learn more about Arrow™ Antimicrobial Catheter and Kitting Solutions, visit our website.

About Teleflex Incorporated

As a global provider of medical technologies, Teleflex is driven by our purpose to improve the health and quality of people’s lives. Through our vision to become the most trusted partner in healthcare, we offer a diverse portfolio with solutions in the therapy areas of anesthesia, emergency medicine, interventional cardiology and radiology, surgical, vascular access, and urology. We believe that the potential of great people, purpose driven innovation, and world-class products can shape the future direction of healthcare.

Teleflex is the home of Arrow™, Barrigel™, Deknatel™, LMA™, Pilling™, QuikClot™, Rüsch™, UroLift™ and Weck™ – trusted brands united by a common sense of purpose.

At Teleflex, we are empowering the future of healthcare. For more information, please visit teleflex.com.

Forward-Looking Statements

Any statements contained in this press release that do not describe historical facts may constitute forward-looking statements. Any forward-looking statements contained herein are based on our management's current beliefs and expectations, but are subject to a number of risks, uncertainties and changes in circumstances, which may cause actual results or company actions to differ materially from what is expressed or implied by these statements. These risks and uncertainties are identified and described in more detail in our filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission, including our Annual Report on Form 10-K.

Contraindication: The Arrowg+ard Blue Plus™ CVC is contraindicated for patients with known hypersensitivity to chlorhexidine and silver sulfadiazine and/or sulfa drugs.

data.title: Apogee Enterprises Raises Quarterly Dividend to 26c From 25c >APOG
Apogee Enterprises Increases Quarterly Dividend >APOG
Apogee Enterprises Increases Quarterly Dividend
data.description:
(11:30 GMT) *Apogee Enterprises Increases Quarterly Dividend >APOG
(11:30 GMT) Press Release: Apogee Enterprises Increases Quarterly Dividend

Apogee Enterprises Increases Quarterly Dividend

Apogee's Twelfth Consecutive Year with a Dividend Increase

MINNEAPOLIS--(BUSINESS WIRE)--January 09, 2025--

Apogee Enterprises, Inc. APOG announced today that its Board of Directors has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.26 per share, a 4% increase from its previous quarterly dividend rate of $0.25 per share. The dividend will be payable on February 12, 2025, to shareholders of record at the close of business on January 28, 2025. This marks Apogee's twelfth consecutive year with a dividend increase, during which time the quarterly dividend has grown by 189%, from $0.09 per share to $0.26 per share.

About Apogee Enterprises, Inc.

Apogee Enterprises, Inc. APOG is a leading provider of architectural building products and services, as well as high-performance coated materials used in a variety of applications. Headquartered in Minneapolis, MN, our portfolio of industry-leading products and services includes architectural glass, windows, curtainwall, storefront and entrance systems, integrated project management and installation services, and high-performance coatings that provide protection, innovative design, and enhanced performance. For more information, visit www.apog.com.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250109089963/en/

CONTACT: Jeff Huebschen

Vice President, Investor Relations & Communications

952.487.7538

ir@apog.com

data.title: Riber Bags New Order from Teledyne Imaging Sensors
data.description:
Riber ALRIB said Thursday it secured a repeat order for its MBE 412 cluster system from Teledyne Imaging Sensors in the US.

The contract will help Teledyne expand its production capacity to fulfill commitments for advanced devices for infrared cameras used in space and terrestrial astronomy.

The latest order follows one placed by Teledyne in July 2024, the French semiconductor equipment maker said.
